input.instructions: Research the design and best practices for capturing web search results and fetched web pages into a personal RAG / vector memory system that augments an AI assistant's long-term memory. Cover concrete prior art (Khoj, Karakeep/hoarder, ArchiveBox, Memex by Worldbrain, Obsidian Smart Connections/Copilot, Open WebUI knowledge, Letta/MemGPT external corpus, GPT-Researcher, Stanford Storm, langchain open_deep_research). For each, what do they actually persist, schema-wise? Then cover engineering best practices in 2025/2026 for: HTML cleaning (trafilatura vs readability vs jusText vs Firecrawl markdown), chunking long-form web pages (semantic vs fixed-window, late chunking, Anthropic contextual retrieval), deduplication (URL canonicalization, content hashing, near-dup via simhash/minhash), re-fetch policy and staleness, hybrid ranking (BM25 + dense + recency + source weight), separate-table vs doc_type-discriminator storage tradeoffs, and pitfalls (corpus pollution, embedding cost, echo-chamber/feedback loops, license/copyright for personal use). Surface contrarian takes from anyone arguing this is a bad idea or low-ROI.

input.instructions: Compare Matrix mobile clients in 2025-2026 by HTML rendering capability. Specifically evaluate Element Android, Element iOS, Element X (new sliding-sync client), SchildiChat (Element fork), FluffyChat, Cinny (PWA), Hydrogen, and any other actively-maintained options. For each, document: (1) Does it render HTML <details>/<summary> as collapsible folds or strip them? (2) Does it apply syntax highlighting to <pre><code class='language-x'> blocks? (3) Does it honor <font color> or only data-mx-color? (4) Any release notes from 2025-2026 announcing improved HTML support? Also: are there iOS/Android wrappers (Capacitor, Tauri, native WebView shells) around Element Web that ship as installable apps? PWA quality on iOS Safari? Recommend the cleanest path to Claude-Desktop-like rich rendering on a phone.

input.instructions: Research state-of-the-art techniques for AI agent episodic memory systems that combine knowledge graphs (FalkorDB, Neo4j) with vector + BM25 stores. Compare what production systems Cognee, Mem0, Letta, Zep, Graphiti, LangGraph Memory, and Microsoft GraphRAG actually implement for: (1) knowledge graph quality - entity extraction, entity resolution and dedup strategies, community detection algorithms (Leiden, Louvain, label propagation, hierarchical), multi-hop ranking with personalized PageRank or GNN scoring, contradiction detection, bi-temporal fact reasoning. (2) Hybrid retrieval beyond RRF - learned-to-rank approaches, ColBERT late-interaction, query rewriting via LLMs before retrieval, semantic chunking strategies, ParadeDB-specific BM25 features for memory. (3) KG-vector integration patterns - joint scoring of graph traversal + vector similarity, graph-expanded query terms, fact provenance back to source, retrieval-augmented community summaries, two-pass retrieval. (4) Scale considerations - what's worth doing at homelab scale (~32K episodes) vs enterprise. Cite specific code paths, GitHub URLs, papers, and practitioner experience. Focus on shipped patterns, not theoretical proposals.

input.instructions:
Compare these models for structured JSON entity-and-relationship extraction (knowledge graph / KG triples) from short text summaries, running on a consumer GPU (RTX 3060 12GB, llama.cpp with --n-cpu-moe expert offload):

1. Gemma 4 26B A4B (Google's latest Gemma MoE, ~26B total / ~4B active params)
2. Latest Qwen models — specifically Qwen3, Qwen3-30B-A3B (if it exists), and any smaller Qwen3 MoE variants in similar weight class
3. Claude Haiku 4.5 (cloud baseline for comparison)

Focus the research on:

A) BENCHMARKS — find published numbers for:
   - BFCL (Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard)
   - IFEval (Instruction Following Eval)
   - MMLU-Pro
   - JSON schema reliability / structured output benchmarks
   - Any KG-extraction-specific evals

B) REAL-WORLD INFERENCE PERFORMANCE on consumer hardware:
   - Tokens/sec on RTX 3060 12GB with llama.cpp
   - Effect of --n-cpu-moe expert offload for MoE models
   - Q4_K_M and Unsloth UD-Q4_K_M quant quality and speed
   - VRAM usage at common context lengths (4K, 8K, 16K)

C) KG MEMORY PIPELINES that have benchmarked local models:
   - Mem0
   - Letta (formerly MemGPT)
   - Graphiti (Zep)
   - Cognee
   - Any other open-source KG/memory frameworks that publish model comparison results

D) PRACTICAL VERDICT:
   The user currently runs Gemma 4 26B A4B at ~25 t/s producing valid JSON for entity/relation extraction from short conversation summaries. Should they switch to a Qwen3 MoE in similar weight class? What are the specific tradeoffs (JSON adherence, KG quality, speed, memory) and is there a clear winner for this narrow task?

Return a structured comparison with citations. Prefer recent (2025-2026) sources, official model cards, llama.cpp/Unsloth GitHub issues, and KG-pipeline benchmark posts.

input.instructions:
Compare these models for structured JSON entity-and-relationship extraction (knowledge graph / KG triples) from short text summaries, with emphasis on practical local deployment on a consumer GPU (RTX 3060 12GB) using llama.cpp with --n-cpu-moe expert offload:

Models to compare:
1. Gemma 3 27B / Gemma 4 26B A4B (Google Gemma family, including any new MoE variant with ~4B active params)
2. Latest Qwen3 dense models (Qwen3 8B, 14B, 32B)
3. Qwen3-30B-A3B (Mixture of Experts, 30B total / 3B active) and any smaller Qwen MoE variants
4. Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 (cloud API, for cost/quality reference)

Specific aspects to research:

A) BENCHMARKS — find published numbers for each model on:
   - BFCL (Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard) — tool/function call accuracy
   - IFEval — instruction following
   - MMLU-Pro — knowledge
   - JSON schema adherence / structured output reliability (any benchmark: JSONSchemaBench, StructEval, etc.)
   - Information extraction / NER / relation extraction benchmarks if available

B) LOCAL INFERENCE PERFORMANCE on RTX 3060 12GB (or similar 12GB consumer GPUs):
   - Real-world tokens/sec with llama.cpp using GGUF quants
   - Specifically for MoE models: --n-cpu-moe / -ot expert offload performance
   - Q4_K_M and unsloth UD-Q4_K_M quant quality vs full precision
   - VRAM usage, context window practical limits
   - Comparison of dense vs MoE throughput when MoE experts are CPU-offloaded

C) KG EXTRACTION PIPELINES that have benchmarked local LLMs:
   - Mem0
   - Letta (formerly MemGPT)
   - Graphiti (Zep)
   - Cognee
   - Any published comparison of local model quality for entity/relation extraction

D) PRACTICAL DECISION:
   The user currently runs Gemma 4 26B A4B (or Gemma 3 27B) at ~25 tokens/sec producing valid JSON for KG triples. Should they switch to Qwen3-30B-A3B (MoE) for better speed via --n-cpu-moe offload? What is the realistic quality tradeoff? Are there quality regressions on JSON / function-calling for Qwen3 MoE compared to Gemma at the same active-parameter count?

Please cite sources (HuggingFace model cards, official blog posts, llama.cpp PRs/issues, r/LocalLLaMA threads, benchmark leaderboards). Prioritize information from 2025-2026.

input.instructions:
Research knowledge graph ontology design for personal-scale memory systems (32K episodes, ~5K entities). Cover:

1. What entity type taxonomies do production KG memory systems use? Specifically Graphiti (Zep AI), Mem0, Cognee, Letta, Neo4j GraphRAG, Microsoft GraphRAG, LlamaIndex KnowledgeGraphIndex. Are entities typed (Person, Tool, Project, Concept, Document) or generic? What's the actual label list?

2. Node schema depth — what properties beyond name/summary do production systems put on nodes? Status, priority, confidence, valid_at, custom domain fields? When does adding properties help retrieval vs become noise?

3. Should codebases have their own ontology distinct from general memory? File/Function/Class/Module/Test entity types vs mixing code references into a generic Entity graph. How do code-RAG systems like Sourcegraph, Aider's repo map, Cursor's index, GitHub Copilot Workspace structure their graphs?

4. Should research/web-source content get its own ontology distinct from conversation-derived entities? Cognee and LlamaIndex KG patterns.

5. Single graph with group_id partitioning vs multiple separate graphs. When does separation pay off?

6. What ontology mistakes do practitioners report? Over-engineering signs vs under-specification signs at single-user scale.

7. Generic Entity + label/type field, vs node label per type (Neo4j multi-label), vs separate node types entirely. Pros and cons in production.
